title: Causal multi-task horizon buckets
summary: Recent lap-level pit-stop research and broader event-sequence/survival literature support predicting both the immediate event and a coarser time-to-event target.
rationale: Recent lap-level pit-stop research and broader event-sequence/survival literature support predicting both the immediate event and a coarser time-to-event target. That usually stabilizes learning better than a single binary head.
implementation_hint: Build a causal GRU/TCN or simple sequence encoder per `Race_Year x Driver` with heads for `PitNextLap` and ordinal `LapsToNextStop` buckets such as `1`, `2`, `3-5`, `6-10`, `>10`; export only OOF predictions for blending with the tabular model.
expected_effect: Should sharpen discrimination around the actual pit window and reduce the tendency to treat all non-next-lap negatives as equally uninformative.
risk: Bidirectional models are tempting but leaky here; sequence models can also underperform strong GBDTs unless the training pipeline is fully causal and fold-safe.
